plan_summary: GradientBoosting (200 estimators, max_depth=4) with engineered features (Title, FamilySize) for Titanic binary classification
reviewer_reasoning: Primary metric (AUC-ROC) regressed from 0.835 to 0.822 (-0.013). While secondary metrics (accuracy +0.017, F1 +0.024, recall +0.029) improved slightly, the model exhibits severe overfitting: train AUC 0.993 vs validation 0.822, a 17.2% gap with a diverging learning curve. The GBM is memorising the training set rather than learning generalisable patterns. The overfitting severity (high) and metric regression make this iteration strictly worse than the baseline on the primary metric. Two model families tried (LogisticRegression, GradientBoosting); 8 iterations remain.
router_reasoning: Iteration 2 degraded the primary metric vs iteration 1 (best). The regression is caused by severe overfitting in the GBM, not by a code bug — the execution succeeded cleanly. Rolling back to iteration 1 as the base. The next plan should retry tree-based models with much stronger regularisation (lower max_depth, fewer estimators, higher min_samples_leaf, lower learning rate) or try RandomForest which is less prone to overfitting. The engineered features (Title, FamilySize) appear useful and should be retained.

risk_flags_summary.evidence: Train/val AUC-ROC gap of 0.171 (17.2%); learning curve diverging; train AUC 0.993 vs val 0.822
